overview.analytical_note: All figures are best estimates from Tier 1–3 sources. Iranian casualties carry significant source disagreement: Hengaw (4,600+ in 16d), HRANA (1,600+), Iran Health Ministry (1,444 killed / 18,551 injured), IDF (1,900+ regime members). Cost ranges reflect source divergence. Conflict ongoing. Data as of 15 March 2026, 11:00 EST. NPR (14 Mar): US spending ~$16.5B through 12 days.

phases_table.trigger: Failed Geneva talks; Ayatollah Khamenei killed

phase1.scope: April–October 2024. First direct state-on-state strikes. Sources: IISS, FDD, IDF, CENTCOM, AP, Reuters.

phase2.scope: 12 days. Iran: 1,190 killed, 4,475 wounded. Israel: 28 killed, 3,491 wounded. Iran fired ~591 BMs + ~1,000 drones. US struck nuclear sites Day 10. Sources: HRANA, Statista, IDF, FDD, ACLED, FPRI.

phase3.source_note: Hengaw (field documentation, 24 provinces): 4,400+ military killed as of Day 15 (up from 4,300+ in 10d). Iran Health Ministry (Mar 14): 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured (civilian). HRANA (verified civilian focus): 1,600+ killed (1,276+ verified civilian, 200+ children). IDF: 1,900+ regime members eliminated. NPR (14 Mar): 1,200+ civilians killed, 10,000+ injured. Divergence reflects different methodologies and access. Hengaw includes unconfirmed military in struck facilities. HRANA verifies individual civilian names. Iran underreports military losses. IDF counts combatant targets only.

phase3.basij_note: Starting ~Mar 11, Israel deployed AI-guided drone swarms against Basij militia checkpoints in Tehran and other cities, targeting internal security forces responsible for protest suppression. The IDF confirmed targeting Basij members using a “mother launcher” platform deploying multiple AI-equipped drones. WSJ reported Iranian civilians transmitted Basij checkpoint locations to Israel via social media. This represents a strategic shift from military infrastructure to internal security apparatus — directly undermining regime coercive capacity and significantly strengthening Scenario C (Regime Fracture) probability. Interception rate Phase III: ~85–90% overall (Israel + Gulf combined); however, Iran’s sheer volume produced significant leakage — at least 19 commercial ships, multiple residential buildings, and military facilities hit.
phase3.day15_note: CENTCOM struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island (Iran’s main oil export hub, ~90% of export crude), destroying naval mine storage, missile bunkers, and military facilities. Oil infrastructure was deliberately preserved. Trump: “destroyed 100% of Iran’s Military capability” at Kharg, warned oil would be next. Iran retaliated with unprecedented Gulf-wide barrage: UAE (9 BMs + 33 drones — Fujairah port fire), Saudi Arabia (6 BMs intercepted al-Kharj; 26 drones downed), Qatar (4 BMs + drones intercepted), Kuwait (airport damaged, Al-Jaber AB radar hit). This represents Iran’s strategic pivot from declining Israel-directed BMs to Gulf-state targeting — a significant escalation. Trump called for warship coalition (US, UK, France, Japan, S. Korea, China) to reopen Hormuz.

Kharg Island: 90+ military targets (CENTCOM). Trump: warship coalition for Hormuz. UAE: 9 BMs + 33 drones (Fujairah fire). Saudi: 6 BMs intercepted. Qatar: 4 BMs. Kuwait airport + Al-Jaber AB radar. Iran BM pivot to Gulf. Oil: $103.14. Hengaw: 4,400+ mil. killed

Heavy airstrikes: Isfahan, Shiraz (20 blasts), S. Tehran, Dezful AB, Khomein, Hamedan. Jask port damage. 7 BM salvos at Israel. 3 wounded Eilat (child). KC-135 crew named. Gharibabadi condemns cultural sites. As of 11:00 EST

scenarios.method: Key assumptions check, driver mapping, comparative analysis (Iran-Iraq War, 2003 Iraq, 2011 Libya), game theory. Updated with 16 days of data. Probabilities are qualitative judgments. Day 15 Kharg strike + Gulf-wide Iranian attacks represent critical inflection point.

options.title: Attritional Stalemate → Negotiated Ceasefire
options.description: US/Israel achieve primary objectives within 3–6 weeks. Iran BM capacity already down 90% toward Israel; however, Iran pivoting remaining munitions to Gulf states (Day 15 barrage). Mojtaba Khamenei consolidates as Supreme Leader but faces pressure. Trump’s warship coalition call signals desire for Hormuz resolution. Kharg Island strike approaches oil infrastructure boundary — warning before final escalation. Pezeshkian’s conditions (recognition, reparations, guarantees) provide diplomatic framework. US faces $1B+/day cost + Hormuz disruption + 14 dead. Ceasefire possible within 4–8 weeks. Nuclear program set back 5–10 years. Oil normalizes to $85–95.

options.title: Prolonged Multi-Front Regional War
options.description: Iran refuses to negotiate. Gulf-wide barrage (Day 15) signals Iran willing to expand war to all neighbors. Fujairah port fire, Kuwait airport damage, Saudi interceptions all indicate Iran’s asymmetric escalation beyond Israel. Mojtaba Khamenei’s Hormuz closure vow holds. Iran redirects remaining BMs from Israel to Gulf economic targets — oil infrastructure next. Hezbollah 1,000+ waves + joint ops continue. Russia expands intel support. 7 BM salvos at Israel Day 16 shows continued capability. Trump’s coalition call risks drawing more nations in. Oil sustains $120+. Conflict 3–6+ months. US cost: $100–250B. Global recession risk significant.

options.title: Regime Fracture & Internal Collapse
options.description: Combined military strikes (7,600+ targets), Ayatollah Khamenei’s death, Kharg military infrastructure destroyed, economic collapse, Dec 2025 protests, wartime disruption (3.2M displaced, 77+ health facilities) destabilize regime. Mojtaba Khamenei — untested — fails to consolidate. IRGC splits between hardliners and pragmatists. Israeli Basij drone strikes erode internal security. Provincial unrest resurges (Baluchistan, Kurdistan, Khuzestan). Elite defections. Iran Health Ministry figures (18,551 injured) indicate healthcare system collapse. Cascading institutional crisis within 2–4 months.
